action.reasoning: Observation: Fine-tuning the entire CLIP ViT-B/16 model resulted in severe overfitting to the source domains (val acc 0.66, test acc 0.22), performing worse than the ResNet50 baseline. Analysis: Fine-tuning a large ViT on a small dataset destroys its pre-trained representations, leading to poor domain generalization. Decision: Switch to DINOv2 ViT-B/14, which is known for excellent frozen features. I will freeze the backbone and only train a linear classifier head. This should preserve the generalizable features and train much faster. I will train for 5 epochs to evaluate.
